rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes; use implementation annexes for bindings
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
The authoritative source for component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections is the input AADL model
generic_rules: Maintain consistent scope and visibility rules when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Ensure all referenced identifiers in AGREE contracts are visible in the target scope
Preserve the original AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions or guarantees in implementation annexes
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL property associations as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
